content.thinking: The cluster has client certs. Let me try connecting using the certs from inside the pod, or using the --certs-dir option. The certs are stored in a secret. Let me check where they're mounted inside the pod.

input.description: Check cert directory inside the pod

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs -e "SET CLUSTER SETTING kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate = '128 MiB';" 2>&1

content.text: Setting applied. Now verify it took effect:

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs -e "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate;" 2>&1

content.text: Confirmed at 128 MiB. Now delete the pod and wait for it to restart:

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb delete pod crdb-cluster-0 2>&1 && kubectl -n cockroachdb wait --for=condition=ready pod/crdb-cluster-0 --timeout=120s 2>&1

content.thinking: The pod is having liveness heartbeat issues. This is expected behavior - when a node restarts, it takes some time to reconnect to the cluster and establish quorum. The node is in the process of starting up. Let me wait longer for it to become ready.

content.text: The node is reconnecting to the cluster after restart — this is normal, just needs more time. Let me poll until it's ready:

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pod crdb-cluster-0 --no-headers 2>&1 | awk '{print $2}')
  echo "$(date): $STATUS"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "1/1" ]; then
    echo "Pod is ready!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
input.description: Poll crdb-cluster-0 until ready (up to 4 minutes)

description: Poll crdb-cluster-0 until ready (up to 4 minutes)

summary: Poll crdb-cluster-0 until ready (up to 4 minutes)

content.thinking: The node has had 1 restart and is still struggling with liveness heartbeat failures. This is likely due to resource contention in the kind cluster. The node is up and running, but not passing the health check. Let me wait a bit more - the liveness issues often resolve themselves as the cluster stabilizes.

content.text: The node restarted once and is still catching up. Let me wait longer with extended polling:

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 36); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pod crdb-cluster-0 --no-headers 2>&1 | awk '{print $2}')
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S): $STATUS"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "1/1" ]; then
    echo "Pod is ready!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
input.description: Poll crdb-cluster-0 until ready (up to 6 more minutes)

description: Poll crdb-cluster-0 until ready (up to 6 more minutes)

content.text: Pod is ready. Now verify the setting persisted after the restart:

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs -e "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate;" 2>&1
input.description: Verify setting persisted after pod restart
